pred_reason: The SWC-107 label is reasonable because multiple findings demonstrate reentrancy vulnerabilities violating the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern, specifically in onSushiReward and reclaimTokens functions where state updates occur after external calls. Evidence: onSushiReward updates user.unpaidRewards after safeTransfer calls; reclaimTokens performs external transfers without lock modifier; reclaimTokens vulnerable to reentrancy via malicious ERC20 tokens

findings.Issue: Reentrancy in onSushiReward due to state changes after external call
findings.Description: The onSushiReward function violates Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern by updating user.unpaidRewards after external token transfers. This vulnerability appears in both the smart contract code and was detected by static analysis.
findings.Impact: Potential reentrancy attacks allowing manipulation of reward calculations, fund loss, or state corruption if reward token is malicious
findings.Location: onSushiReward function, lines with safeTransfer calls followed by user.unpaidRewards updates; Static analysis reentrancy-no-eth findings

findings.Issue: Missing lock modifier on reclaimTokens function
findings.Description: The reclaimTokens function performs external transfers without the lock modifier, creating reentrancy vulnerability. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential reentrancy attacks allowing fund theft or state manipulation if called with malicious token contracts
findings.Location: reclaimTokens function definition missing lock modifier

findings.Issue: Reentrancy risk in reclaimTokens with malicious tokens
findings.Description: The reclaimTokens function is vulnerable to reentrancy when handling malicious ERC20 tokens, despite onlyOwner restriction. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential loss of all contract funds if owner is compromised or tricked into calling with malicious token
findings.Location: reclaimTokens function with external safeTransfer calls

findings.Issue: Missing onlyMCV2 modifier on pendingTokens function
findings.Description: The pendingTokens function lacks the onlyMCV2 modifier, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive reward data. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized access to pending reward information, potential data scraping for manipulation
findings.Location: pendingTokens function definition missing onlyMCV2 modifier

findings.Issue: Missing zero-address validation in constructor
findings.Description: The constructor lacks validation for _MASTERCHEF_V2 parameter, allowing zero address assignment. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Contract bricking if zero address is passed, requiring redeployment
findings.Location: AmunRewarder constructor; Static analysis missing-zero-check finding

findings.Issue: Missing zero-address validation for to parameter in reclaimTokens
findings.Description: The reclaimTokens function lacks zero-address validation for the to parameter, risking irreversible fund loss. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Permanent loss of funds if to address is set to zero
findings.Location: reclaimTokens function; Static analysis missing-zero-check finding

findings.Issue: Missing zero-address validation in init function parameters
findings.Description: The init function lacks zero-address validation for owner and masterLpToken parameters. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential contract bricking or loss of administrative control if zero addresses are set
findings.Location: init function parameter decoding section

findings.Issue: Unprotected division operations in pendingToken function
findings.Description: The pendingToken function performs division operations without BoringMath protection, risking precision loss. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Inaccurate reward calculations leading to unfair distribution or dust accumulation
findings.Location: pendingToken function division operations (/ lpSupply and / ACC_TOKEN_PRECISION)

findings.Issue: Precision loss in accToken1PerShare calculation
findings.Description: Division in accToken1PerShare calculation may round down to zero for small rewards or large LP supply. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Loss of reward accuracy, systematic underpayment of small rewards over time
findings.Location: updatePool and pendingToken functions, sushiReward.mul(ACC_TOKEN_PRECISION) / lpSupply

findings.Issue: Miner-influenced timestamp manipulation in reward calculations
findings.Description: Block.timestamp usage in updatePool and pendingToken allows miner manipulation within limits. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Potential reward amount manipulation within ~15 minute range, affecting fairness
findings.Location: updatePool and pendingToken functions; Static analysis timestamp findings
